docs.pageSubtitle: Everything you need to know about astro-intl

hero.title: Get Started with
hero.description: A <bold>type-safe</bold> internationalization library for Astro, inspired by next-intl. No props drilling, dynamic loading, and full TypeScript support.

installation.title: Installation
installation.description: Install astro-intl using your preferred package manager.
installation.addIntegration: Optionally register the integration in your astro.config.mjs:
installation.integrationNote: The integration is optional — it only logs a startup message. The core API works without it.

fileStructure.title: File Structure
fileStructure.description: astro-intl expects a specific folder structure inside your project. Here is the recommended layout:
fileStructure.pagesNote: Pages must be nested under a [lang] dynamic segment so Astro can generate one route per locale.
fileStructure.messagesNote: Each locale gets its own JSON file inside src/i18n/messages/. The filename must match the locale code exactly (e.g. en.json, es.json).
fileStructure.requestNote: The request.ts file exports a function that receives the locale string and returns the messages for that locale.

configuration.title: Configuration
configuration.messagesDescription: Create one JSON file per locale inside src/i18n/messages/. You can organize keys into namespaces (nested objects).
configuration.approachDescription: astro-intl offers two ways to load messages. Pick the one that fits your project best.
configuration.noMiddlewareNote: If you are not using createIntlMiddleware, you must pass defaultLocale and locales directly in astro.config.mjs so that functions like getLocales(), getDefaultLocale(), and isValidLocale() work correctly.
configuration.simpleDescription: Pass your message imports along with defaultLocale and locales directly in astro.config.mjs. No extra files needed.
configuration.advancedDescription: For full control, use defineRequestConfig to create a request file — similar to next-intl. This lets you add custom logic like fetching messages from a CMS. You still need to pass defaultLocale and locales in astro.config.mjs.
configuration.layoutDescription: Best practice: Call setRequestLocale once in a shared layout (e.g., src/layouts/Layout.astro) instead of repeating it on every page. This initializes the global i18n context before any component reads translations.
configuration.middlewareDescription: Instead of calling setRequestLocale in every layout, you can use createIntlMiddleware to handle it automatically for all routes. This also sets locales and defaultLocale in the store, so you don't need to pass them in astro.config.mjs.
configuration.staticPathsDescription: In every [lang] page, export getStaticPaths returning all supported locales so Astro can pre-render them.

usage.title: Usage
usage.basicDescription: Once your layout is configured, simply use getTranslations(namespace) in any page to get a typed translation function for a specific namespace.
usage.interpolationDescription: Use {curlyOpen}varName{curlyClose} placeholders in your translation strings and pass a values object as the second argument to t(). Accepted types: string, number, boolean. Missing or null/undefined values keep the placeholder unchanged.
usage.markupDescription: Use t.markup(key, tags) to interpolate HTML tags inside a translation string. The tags object maps tag names to functions that receive the inner content.
usage.markupInterpolationDescription: Combine variable interpolation with tag replacement by passing {curlyOpen} values, tags {curlyClose} to t.markup(). Interpolation runs first, then tags are processed.
usage.reactDescription: Use getTranslationsReact(namespace) inside React components, then call t.rich(key, tags) to interpolate React nodes.

setRequestLocale.name: setRequestLocale
setRequestLocale.description: Extracts the locale from the URL pathname (first segment) and calls your getConfig function to load messages. Must be awaited before any other call.

getLocale.name: getLocale
getLocale.description: Returns the current locale string set by setRequestLocale. Throws if called before setting the locale.

getTranslations.name: getTranslations
getTranslations.description: Returns a typed t(key, values?) function scoped to the given namespace. The optional values object replaces {curlyOpen}varName{curlyClose} placeholders. Also exposes t.markup(key, tags | {curlyOpen} values?, tags {curlyClose}) for HTML interpolation with optional variable substitution.

getTranslationsReact.name: getTranslationsReact
getTranslationsReact.description: Same as getTranslations but returns a function with t.rich(key, tags) for React node interpolation.

requestConfig.name: RequestConfig
requestConfig.description: Type returned by your getRequestConfig function. Contains locale: string and messages: Record.

primitive.name: Primitive
primitive.description: Union type for interpolation values: string | number | boolean | null | undefined. Used as the value type in the values object passed to t() and t.markup().

runWithLocale.name: runWithLocale
runWithLocale.signature: runWithLocale<R>(url: URL, fn: () => R | Promise<R>, getConfig?: GetRequestConfigFn): Promise<R>
runWithLocale.description: Runs a function inside an isolated per-request context. Uses AsyncLocalStorage on Node.js for concurrency-safe SSR. Falls back to a global variable on runtimes without it (Cloudflare Workers, Deno).

intlConfig.name: IntlConfig
intlConfig.description: Configuration type for the integration options. Contains defaultLocale: string — the locale used when the URL has no language prefix.

getDefaultLocale.name: getDefaultLocale
getDefaultLocale.description: Returns the defaultLocale configured via the integration options or createIntlMiddleware. Defaults to "en" if not set.

getLocales.name: getLocales
getLocales.description: Returns the array of locales configured via the integration options or createIntlMiddleware. Returns an empty array if not set.

isValidLocale.name: isValidLocale
isValidLocale.description: Checks whether a locale string is in the configured locales list. Returns true for any string if no locales have been configured.

getMessages.name: getMessages
getMessages.description: Returns the full messages object for the current request. Throws if called before setRequestLocale.

createIntlMiddleware.name: createIntlMiddleware
createIntlMiddleware.signature: createIntlMiddleware(options: { locales: string[]; defaultLocale?: string; routes?: RoutesMap })
createIntlMiddleware.description: Creates an Astro middleware that automatically calls setRequestLocale on every request. Also sets locales, defaultLocale, and routes in the intl store. When routes are provided, the middleware rewrites translated URLs to their canonical filesystem paths. Import from astro-intl/middleware.

path.name: path
path.signature: path(routeKey: string, options?: { locale?: string; params?: Record<string, string>; encode?: boolean }): string
path.description: Generates a localized URL for a named route. Uses the current locale if none is specified. Substitutes [param] placeholders with the provided params. Import from astro-intl/routing.

switchLocalePath.name: switchLocalePath
switchLocalePath.signature: switchLocalePath(currentPath: string | URL, nextLocale: string): string
switchLocalePath.description: Converts the current URL to its equivalent in another locale. Matches the path against route templates, extracts dynamic params, and rebuilds the URL using the target locale's template. Preserves query strings and hashes. Import from astro-intl/routing.

dotPaths.name: DotPaths<T>
dotPaths.description: Utility type that generates all valid dot-notation paths for a given messages object. Used internally to provide autocomplete for translation keys.

routesMap.name: RoutesMap
routesMap.description: Type for the routes configuration object. Maps route keys to an object of locale → URL template pairs. Example: { about: { en: "/about", es: "/sobre-nosotros" } }.

routing.title: Routing
routing.intro: astro-intl provides a routing system that lets you define translated URL paths for each locale. This enables localized URLs like /es/sobre-nosotros instead of /es/about.
routing.defineRoutesDescription: Add a routes map to your routing config. Each key is a route name, and each value maps locales to their URL templates. Use [param] for dynamic segments.
routing.withMiddlewareDescription: When using createIntlMiddleware, pass your routes in the config. The middleware will automatically rewrite translated URLs to their canonical filesystem paths — no extra page files needed.
routing.withMiddlewareStep2: Your filesystem only needs the default locale paths:
routing.withMiddlewareHowDescription: When a user visits /es/sobre-nosotros, the middleware matches it against the routes map, finds the canonical template (/about), and rewrites the request to /es/about — which maps to your [lang]/about.astro file. No duplicate pages needed.
routing.withoutMiddlewareDescription: If you prefer not to use middleware, you can still use path() and switchLocalePath() for URL generation. Configure routes via the integration options instead.
routing.withoutMiddlewareNote: Without middleware, there are no automatic rewrites. You need to create page files for each translated path. The simplest approach is to create thin wrapper pages that re-export the canonical one.
routing.withoutMiddlewareExampleTitle: Example: Astro native routing for translated paths
routing.withoutMiddlewareExampleDescription: Create a page file for each translated URL. These "wrapper" pages simply re-export everything from the canonical (default locale) page — zero duplication.
routing.withoutMiddlewareExampleStep2: Create the translated route as a thin wrapper:
routing.withoutMiddlewareExampleNote: Each wrapper file is just 2 lines. The canonical page contains all the logic. When Astro builds /es/sobre-nosotros, it renders the same component as /es/about — the translated URL works without middleware.
routing.pathDescription: Use path(routeKey, options?) to generate localized URLs. It picks the correct template for the target locale and substitutes any params.
routing.switchDescription: Use switchLocalePath(currentPath, nextLocale) to convert the current URL to its equivalent in another locale. It matches the current path against route templates, extracts params, and rebuilds the URL with the target locale's template.
routing.switchNote: If the current path doesn't match any route template, switchLocalePath falls back to simply swapping the locale prefix.
routing.paramsDescription: Route templates support dynamic segments with [param] syntax. You can have multiple params per route — they are extracted and substituted automatically.

examples.title: Examples
examples.namespaceDescription: Namespaces let you split large translation files into logical sections. Pass the namespace name to getTranslations.
examples.interpolationDescription: Replace {curlyOpen}varName{curlyClose} placeholders in your translations with dynamic values at runtime.
examples.typeDescription: Pass your messages type as a generic to getTranslations to get full autocomplete and type checking on translation keys.
examples.localeDescription: Use getLocale() anywhere after setRequestLocale has been called — for example to build locale-aware URLs.
